explanation: The property describes a sequence of events triggered by an HELLO message being sent. The initial event, 'a generic DM_i sends an HELLO message to OLSR', is represented by the atomic proposition 'hello_msg_sent_dm_to_local_node'. The subsequent actions, 'Local Node must forward the HELLO message to Link Set', 'to Neighbor Set that update their information', and 'to MPR Set, which activates a recalculation', imply a causal and temporal chain. Each 'then' and 'finally' indicates that the following event must eventually occur after the previous one. This nested eventual sequence is captured using the 'F' (finally) operator. Specifically, if the initial HELLO message occurs, then eventually the message is forwarded to Link Set. After that, eventually the message is forwarded to Neighbor Set and an update is performed. Finally, after that, eventually the message is sent to MPR Set and a recalculation is activated. This entire chained implication is expressed as 'hello_msg_sent_dm_to_local_node -> F(msg_forwarded_to_link_set & F((msg_forwarded_to_neighbor_set & update_performed_by_neighbor_set) & F(msg_forwarded_to_mpr_set & recalculation_performed_by_mpr_set)))'. The phrase 'This property holds for every DM_i' indicates that this entire sequence must hold globally at all times, hence the 'G' (globally) operator is applied to the entire formula.
